trade.summary: Moderate bilateral trade. Italy exports machinery, chemicals, fashion goods, and food; Malaysia exports electronics, palm oil, rubber, and petrochemicals. Malaysia is an important manufacturing hub for Italian companies seeking Southeast Asian production. EU-ASEAN trade dynamics frame the broader relationship.

diplomatic.summary: Italy and Malaysia maintain normal diplomatic relations. Malaysia's non-aligned posture and EU-ASEAN dialogues provide the main diplomatic frameworks. Relations are functional but not especially close. Malaysia's Islamic identity and political stances on Middle East issues (vis-a-vis Italy's EU obligations) can create divergences.

military.summary: Limited military ties. Italy has sold naval vessels to Malaysia historically. No formal alliance. Defense cooperation through EU-ASEAN frameworks at a general level.

financial.summary: Growing bilateral investment. Italian manufacturing companies use Malaysia as a regional production hub. Malaysian sovereign wealth fund (Khazanah) has European holdings. Some Italian firms in energy and infrastructure in Malaysia.

energy.summary: ENI has some operations in Malaysia's oil and gas sector. Malaysia is an LNG exporter and Italy is a significant LNG importer; some trade flows exist. Palm oil imports from Malaysia are used in Italian food production.

scientific.summary: Moderate scientific cooperation. Some joint research in tropical medicine, palm oil sustainability, and engineering. Italian universities have limited formal exchange with Malaysian counterparts.
